payload.text:
🔵 SYSTEM PROMPT (paste this into Google AI Studio)

You are the official AI assistant for UpwardEase, a nonprofit platform designed to help students share their stress-coping experiences through video submissions. Your primary mission is to empower students with emotional support, help them express their stories clearly, and assist the UpwardEase team in managing content safely and compassionately.

1. Your Role
	•	Act as a supportive, non-clinical guide that helps students reflect on their experiences.
	•	Provide emotionally safe, encouraging, and age-appropriate communication.
	•	Generate clear, structured, positive written outputs related to video submissions, coping methods, and featured topics.
	•	Assist admins with content analysis, safety checks, tagging, and approval suggestions.

（中文解释：设定 AI 角色为 UpwardEase 官方助手，强调支持性、非医疗、有同理心，同时能帮 admin 审核内容。）

⸻

2. Platform Description (for context)

UpwardEase includes:
	1.	Video Submission Area – any student can upload stress-coping videos.
	2.	Video Gallery – only admin-approved videos appear publicly.
	3.	Weekly Featured Topic – the website highlights one mental-health-related theme each week (e.g., “Exam Stress,” “Homesickness,” “Time Management Burnout”).

（中文解释：让模型理解你的网站结构，这是 AI Studio 很重要的上下文。）

⸻

3. Your Tasks (AI outputs you must support)

A. For Students (creators)
You can help students with:
	•	Drafting a clear video description
	•	Summarizing their coping experience
	•	Giving gentle suggestions for safe expression
	•	Helping them organize their content before recording
	•	Generating tags, keywords, and categories
	•	Encouraging them with supportive tone

Always avoid:
	•	Clinical diagnosis
	•	Judging or dismissing feelings
	•	Giving medical treatment instructions

（中文解释：学生端功能是内容引导和文案生成。）

⸻

B. For Admins
Provide:
	•	A summary of the uploaded video
	•	A sentiment analysis (positive/neutral/sensitive)
	•	Safety notes if the content involves potential red flags
	•	Tag recommendations
	•	A publication recommendation: approve / needs review / do not publish
	•	Clean rewritten text if needed (no censorship, just clarity)

Never:
	•	Fabricate incidents
	•	Mislabel feelings
	•	Make harmful assumptions

（中文解释：管理员端功能是内容审核与标记，但必须真实，不能乱编。）

⸻

C. Featured Topic Assistant
For each weekly featured topic:
	•	Provide a concise definition
	•	Explain why it matters for students
	•	Suggest 3–5 discussion prompts
	•	Give video ideas students can follow
	•	Generate website copy for the weekly homepage section

（中文解释：帮助你每周做 Homepage Featured Topic 的内容。）

⸻

4. Output Format Requirements

When responding, structure your output clearly:

1. Summary  
2. Emotional Tone Analysis  
3. Key Themes  
4. Suggested Tags  
5. Recommended Actions (student/admin)  
6. Optional Improvements  

For student-facing tasks:
Use warm, encouraging, simple language.

For admin-facing tasks:
Use objective, concise, structured, operational tone.

（中文解释：固定格式便于你将来接 API 自动识别字段。）

⸻

5. Safety & Ethics Requirements

You must ensure:
	•	Supportive tone
	•	No harmful advice
	•	No moral judgment
	•	No diagnosis
	•	No fabrication
	•	No instructions involving self-harm
	•	Avoid triggering descriptions
	•	Encourage reaching out to safe adults when needed

If content includes unsafe elements, output:
“This content includes potentially sensitive emotional expressions. Recommend admin review before publishing.”

（中文解释：确保 AI 输出对于心理健康内容足够安全。）

⸻

6. Example Behaviors

If a student uploads a video about exam stress:
	•	Generate tags: exam stress, study pressure, coping skills, teen mental health
	•	Summary: “This video discusses strategies the student uses to handle heavy exam workloads.”
	•	Tone: “Reflective, slightly anxious, but hopeful.”
	•	Suggestion: “Encourage the student to add one practical tip to complete the message.”

（中文解释：提供给模型行为示例，让它更稳定。）

**Initiating the Analysis**

I'm deep into dissecting the UpwardEase request. My immediate focus is on grasping the core of this "world-class" React app. The emphasis on video-sharing for stress coping offers a fascinating angle. I'm visualizing the functionalities and the intended audience, and am thinking about the best ways to incorporate the nonprofit aspect into the app design.


**Refining the Feature Set**

I've been drilling down on the specific features for UpwardEase. The AI assistant and image editing capabilities are starting to gel, and I'm particularly excited about integrating real-time voice interaction. Grounding the app with Google Search and Maps is also becoming a key aspect. I'm prioritizing user experience and focusing on the Admin dashboard's analytical capabilities too.


**Finalizing Tech Stack**

I'm solidifying the tech stack now. Focusing on the Gemini API for strict SDK method adherence is crucial. I've scrapped `react-dropzone` and am prioritizing a responsive, accessible, mobile-first design. The single XML output format is the goal, with `metadata.json` taking precedence. The file structure is also nailed down with `index.tsx`, `index.html`, `App.tsx`, `components/`, and `services/`.


**Structuring the Architecture**

I'm now deep-diving into the component breakdown and services. The `geminiService.ts` is solidifying as the central hub for all the `@google/genai` interactions, neatly encapsulating the API logic. I'm focusing on the modularity of components to facilitate easy updates. I've designed `App.tsx` for layout, and other components for distinct features, including the crucial simulation of admin submission review. I'm excited about how it's shaping up.


**Developing Core Interactions**

I'm now implementing the key interactions. The `streamChat` functionality for the chatbot is taking shape. I'm building out the image editing pipeline, from input to Gemini processing. I am focusing on the Live API, considering the need for WebSockets/AudioContext and bi-directional streaming. I have created "Thinking Mode" and search/maps integration with the chatbot.


**Refining Code Implementation**

I'm presently building the live API using AudioContext for the streaming. I'm focusing on buffer management and processing within the `onmessage` handler. I've added camera/microphone permissions in `metadata.json`, and incorporated the Tailwind CDN script in `index.html`. For image generation and editing, I'm setting up the canvas display, prompt input and model selection based on the process. I am considering the use of `process.env.API_KEY` when initializing the `GoogleGenAI`.


**Adjusting Core Elements**

I'm making a few adjustments. I've integrated the new SDK import into the project. The components are taking shape: a simple navbar, the complex Live API component adapted from the example, combined Gen and Edit image tools, a chat assistant with Search/Maps grounding and "Thinking Mode," and the submission helper. I realized I needed to ensure the system prompt is used within the `geminiService`, so I've added that. I've also selected the correct model for the Live API and added tool configs for Maps/Search.


**Refining Service Logic**

The service needs clearer distinction between "generate" and "edit" image tasks. The image handling uses an input file now, replacing `react-dropzone`. I'm verifying all imports are explicit and that Tailwind classes are mobile-first for responsiveness. I've added basic error handling for API keys and will manage API key selection, defaulting to `process.env.API_KEY` for simplicity as requested, despite more specific API selection prompts.

**Considering AI Integration**

I'm currently focused on integrating the Google GenAI SDK to empower "UpwardEase." My immediate priority is implementing text-to-image editing using `gemini-2.5`. This nano-banana-powered app feature seems straightforward, but I want to ensure it's efficient and user-friendly, setting the stage for more complex AI functionalities.


**Prioritizing Features & Tools**

I'm now prioritizing the integration of various AI tools for UpwardEase.  Specifically, I'm focusing on features like real-time conversations via Gemini 2.5 Native Audio and leveraging Google Search and Maps data. I'm also planning to utilize `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` for image generation, offering users control over image resolution. Plus, I need to integrate the System Prompt to define UpwardEase's role as a helpful assistant to students and admins.


**Defining Core Functionality**

I'm now fully immersed in developing the foundational structure for UpwardEase. The unexpected hiccup has pushed me to start almost from scratch, which, I must say, has clarified the vision. I'm focusing on crafting a responsive UI with a vital sidebar for easy navigation. This forms the base to build the core features. I'm prioritizing user-friendliness, ensuring seamless switching between modes/features. Next I'll develop the video-watching, Q&A, and AI assistant components.


**Defining Module Architecture**

I've started dissecting the architecture for each module in UpwardEase.  The focus now is refining the Student Assistant's Chatbot.  I'm exploring how `gemini-3-pro-preview` can best provide supportive, non-clinical responses to student concerns, especially complex anxiety-related questions.  For the Admin Console, I'm working out a text-based input system for analyzing video descriptions, considering safety checks and content analysis. I'm also planning for the "Creative Studio" to generate images suitable for video thumbnails and mood boards, aiming to ground this with Maps/Search for finding resources, as well as a "Live Support" feature for voice conversation.


**Designing Image Features**

I'm now zeroing in on the "Creative Studio," specifically image generation and editing.  Text prompts are the UI, with aspect ratio and size selectors (1K, 2K, 4K) for user control.  I need to integrate `window.aistudio.openSelectKey()` for this "pro" model, as the instructions state. This aligns with a similar approach used for Veo's paid key requirement. This feature will use `gemini-3-pro-image-preview`, to produce images for video thumbnails and mood boards.


**Integrating New Modules**

I'm now integrating new modules into UpwardEase.  Specifically, I'm focusing on the "Creative Studio" and "Live Support" features. The studio uses `gemini-2.5-flash-image` for image editing with a text prompt input (e.g., "Add a retro filter").  "Live Support" features the  `gemini-2.5-flash-native-audio-preview-09-2025` model, aiming to provide a direct voice conversation interface with the UpwardEase assistant, using a system prompt to define its role. I've also incorporated `googleSearch` and `googleMaps` for the "Find Help" feature, allowing users to locate nearby resources or search for coping strategies.


**Mapping Interface Elements**

I'm now focusing on mapping out the interface elements for UpwardEase.  The structure will include StudentView with its chat, AdminView with its analysis form, StudioView housing image generation/editing, LiveView for the live API connection, and ResourcesView for grounding tools. Tailwind CSS is preferred for styling, assuming it's available, but I'll use inline styles or standard CSS-in-JS objects for rendering robustness if necessary, ensuring a world-class look. This should be a straightforward application of standard HTML/CSS structuring or Tailwind classes, focusing on the interface layout.


**Codifying API Integration**

I'm now fully coding the API integration and handling for UpwardEase.  I'm making sure to use `process.env.API_KEY` for standard API calls and `window.aistudio` for Pro Image Gen with user-selected keys.  I'm meticulously checking off each specific prompt requirement: `gemini-2.5-flash-image` for editing, `gemini-2.5-native-audio` for Live API, and so on.  I'm also setting the `thinkingBudget: 32768` for complex queries.  Since there's only one `.tsx` file, I will consolidate all code here, ensuring a modular and clean structure using sub-components. Let's start the code!


**Fixing Import Statement**

I'm now revising my import statements to correct the `SchemaType` error. I'm focusing on ensuring the correct use of `Type` for the Google GenAI library, ensuring the rest of the code is also accurate. This is an essential step to move the development process forward.


**Finalizing Feature Logic**

My current focus is on building the specific logic for features, ensuring they are seamless and performant.  I'm integrating Live API connections with a connect/disconnect button and audio visualization. I'm also developing the search bar and location toggle for the "Resources" section, allowing users to find local support. I'm adding `thinkingBudget` to the Student Chat and Admin analysis for safety and complex queries. I will refine the image editing with `gemini-2.5-flash-image` and develop a dedicated Resource Finder section. I've also added the `microphone` and `geolocation` to `metadata.json`, and I'm choosing a calm, blue/green/white color scheme for a mental health-friendly look. Lastly, I'm setting up the dependencies.


**Constructing UI & Features**

I'm now deep in the process of building the UI and feature logic for UpwardEase. I'm focusing on ensuring a smooth user experience across all modules. I'm prioritizing the integration of Lucide icons and Tailwind CSS, to maintain a consistent aesthetic. Furthermore, I've incorporated system prompts and the API key checks for the image generation API. I'm using `navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition` for map functionality. Lastly, I'm working with the logic of the code using the given structure.


**Building Final Components**

I'm now integrating the final components into UpwardEase, refining the overall application. The sidebar navigation is complete. I've finished the main content switching based on the active tab. For Live View, I implemented the AudioContext and ScriptProcessor, decoding and processing audio as per the guidelines. Studio View's generation and editing features now function correctly. The resource search logic handles both Google Maps and Google Search. Admin and Student Views are utilizing the `gemini-3-pro-preview` model with the specified thinking budget. I'm focusing on user testing for seamless operation.


**Updating Module Integrations**

I'm currently working to integrate the microphone and geolocation permissions. I've also incorporated a simplified video submission area by creating a file upload field. For the image editing feature, I'm focusing on ensuring users can upload an image, enter text prompts, and receive the edited image. I'm building a dedicated "Find Help" section, with a clear focus on integrating Maps and Search functionality. Finally, I've updated the Student and Admin modes with the System Prompt, ensuring consistency with the overall application's design. I'm confident about these next crucial steps.


**Integrating API Keys**

I'm now implementing the mandatory API key selection for image generation.  I've added a check within the image generation component that directs users to enter their own key when utilizing the `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` model.  The flow mirrors the Veo instructions, ensuring user control and compliance. The coding process is underway, focusing on incorporating the key validation and appropriate error handling.

entries.description: Add permissions for microphone and geolocation.
Implement UpwardEase app with Student, Admin, Studio, Live, and Resources features using Gemini models.

**Developing Feature Set**

I'm currently focused on feature analysis for the UpwardEase app. Based on the files, I need to begin implementing several enhancements, including one relating to image editing capabilities. The key aspects have been extracted, and I'm updating my initial analysis accordingly. I will now integrate this analysis into the codebase.


**Implementing New Features**

I've been occupied by the integration of two fresh features. Firstly, I'm working on enabling text-prompted image editing within the Creative Studio, utilizing the `gemini-2.5-flash-image` model. The user interface for this is largely defined and will provide an upload, text field, and result preview. Secondly, I'm developing a live conversational voice app using the `gemini-2.5-flash-native-audio-preview-09-2025` Live API, which will be implemented within `LiveView`. I will next start implementing Google Search Grounding to enhance both the chatbot and resources views.


**Enhancing Resource View**

I'm now incorporating Google Search grounding into `ResourcesView` to enable users to find up-to-date mental health information. I'm also adding Google Maps grounding to that view, using `gemini-2.5-flash` with the `googleMaps` tool, so that users can easily locate nearby clinics and support groups. Furthermore, the `StudioView` will gain image generation capabilities through `gemini-3-pro-image-preview`, offering different image sizes. I will also review the existing use of Gemini models across the app to ensure they're appropriately using `gemini-2.5-flash` for speed and `gemini-3-pro-preview` for complexity.


**Expanding App Functionality**

I'm presently working on implementing the AI-powered chatbot, leveraging the `gemini-3-pro-preview` model within `StudentView`. This will involve integrating a chat interface for user questions, and also adding a "Deep Think" toggle to enable more complex query handling via the same model, with a `thinkingBudget` of 32768. I'm also planning required adjustments for the imports and state management within `index.tsx`.


**Adding Admin Tools**

I'm now focused on the Admin view, implementing video analysis capabilities. While direct video upload isn't feasible, I'll allow admins to input video descriptions or transcripts for analysis. This will leverage `gemini-3-pro-preview` to generate summaries, sentiment analysis, safety checks, and relevant tags based on the provided text, fulfilling the admin's need to "analyze content". Simultaneously, I'll keep the home view and image generation capabilities in the studio view as is.


**Incorporating Audio & Location**

I'm now integrating the Live API features and location services. I'm focusing on audio streaming within `LiveView`, using the provided system instructions and managing connection and chunking. Simultaneously, I'm integrating Google Search and Maps grounding into `ResourcesView` to facilitate users finding information and clinics with the gemini-2.5-flash model. Also updating the `metadata.json` to include microphone and geolocation permissions. I'm also adding camera, just in case.


**Implementing Audio Stream**

I'm now setting up the audio streaming functionality. I need to establish the `AudioContext`, integrating `ai.live.connect` and managing callbacks for `onopen`, `onmessage`, and `onclose`. I'm using a worklet or script processor to handle mic input. I'll make sure the `StudioView` calls `ai.models.generateContent` with `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` for image generation, with appropriate configurations. Also the edit function will call `gemini-2.5-flash-image`, for the user uploaded image. Then the `ResourcesView` will use the user's input, to call `ai.models.generateContent` with the specified tools. Finally, I'll update the `StudentView`, so the chat interface uses `ai.chats.create` with `gemini-3-pro-preview` based on user input, and the "Deep Thinking" checkbox appropriately configures the `thinkingBudget`. And for the `AdminView`, I have to call `gemini-3-pro-preview` with the proper System prompt.


**Revising Core App Logic**

I'm now revising `index.tsx` to include the new views and ensure proper state management and imports.  I'll start by rewriting the file fully to include all features and avoid issues with the incomplete original.  I must make sure to utilize `GoogleGenAI` and `process.env.API_KEY` for the API calls. I am also making sure to copy the Live API's code patterns, to establish a functional session. I will also incorporate grounding display logic as required by the latest changes. Finally, I will ensure that the correct models are used, as summarized above.


**Assembling the App Structure**

I'm now focused on the final structure and implementation, especially regarding state management and API integration within `index.tsx`. The existing file was incomplete and requires full reconstruction to incorporate all features, so I'll create a completely new structure. I'll make sure to use `GoogleGenAI` and the `API_KEY` environment variable. Also, I will integrate the Live API code patterns to establish a functioning session. Grounding display logic will be handled as per the specifications, and correct models will be used throughout.


**Starting Code Implementation**

I'm now diving into the actual coding phase. My focus is on implementing the structural elements within `index.tsx`, ensuring all functionalities are correctly integrated. This includes incorporating API calls for image generation with various models, Google search, Maps, Live API, and conversational flows, all while adhering to state management principles and security best practices. I'm also ensuring the code is modular and testable, adhering to DRY principles.

Complete implementation of UpwardEase with Student Chat (Thinking), Admin Analysis, Studio (Image Gen/Edit), Live API, and Resources (Search/Maps).

payload.text:
Fix the following errors:
Error: Minified React error #299; visit https://react.dev/errors/299 for the full message or use the non-minified dev environment for full errors and additional helpful warnings.

**Diagnosing React Issue**

I've homed in on error #299, which usually boils down to React failing to find a valid DOM element to render into. Examining the provided `index.tsx` file, the code snippet seems correct in its attempt to grab the "root" element. I'm now checking if that "root" ID is indeed present in the actual HTML structure where the app is rendered. I suspect the issue lies in the HTML or how it's being served.


**Pinpointing the Root Cause**

I've determined the issue. The critical `<div id="root">` element is missing from the provided `index.html` structure. Without it, React's rendering fails, leading to the "Target container is not a DOM element" error. I now need to add a complete HTML structure, including the necessary root div, to address the problem. The importmap seems fine, but without a target, it's irrelevant. I'm moving toward providing a complete example of the `index.html` file.


**Providing the Solution**

The path to resolving this problem is clear: adding the missing `<div id="root">` to `index.html`. Furthermore, to ensure full functionality, a script tag needs insertion to import and execute the application. After reviewing the provided importmap, I've confirmed that the HTML structure is incomplete. My focus now is on constructing a complete, functional `index.html` file that includes the necessary root element and the import script. Finally, I'll confirm no errors remain in the `index.tsx` based on the given prompt.


**Validating HTML Structure**

I'm now fully immersed in reconstructing `index.html`. While some environments might inject the script automatically, a standard HTML file requires it explicitly. My focus remains on including `<div id="root">` for error resolution. I'm building out a valid HTML5 document to be standard and avoid edge cases, including the import map, the root div, and, just to be thorough, the script import as well. Additionally, reviewing `index.tsx` triggered a review of image configurations, confirming correct model and config are set. I will confirm the structure is complete.


**Constructing the index.html**

I'm now putting together the `index.html` file to ensure the React app renders. The previously missing root `div` element is vital, and I'm ensuring the HTML structure is complete, including the necessary importmap script. I'm double-checking the code snippets for any conflicts. I'm confident that a complete HTML file will resolve the rendering error. This HTML construction is the last remaining step.


**Examining Importmap Validity**

I'm now carefully examining the provided `index.html` structure. While the root `div` is present, I'm verifying the CDN's support for the importmap's specific react-dom path. The structure looks complete, but I'm confirming that the nested `react-dom/client` import is valid with the stated CDN path to ensure the application renders successfully. I need to make sure the app will find the `createRoot` dependency correctly.


**Addressing Importmap Detail**

I'm focusing now on the import map. Since `index.tsx` uses `react-dom/client`, I'm adding it explicitly to ensure no pathing discrepancies. I'm also including the tailwindcss script to address its classes. I'm double-checking the direct error message, confirming it relates to the missing DOM element. I'll make the updates now to resolve this quickly.



**Refining HTML and TypeScript**

I'm now putting together the `index.html` file to be sure the React app renders. The root `div` is there and the structure is complete. While examining the provided snippets, I'm incorporating the `index.tsx` details for TailwindCSS and double-checking for conflicts. The HTML structure is almost finished. I'm checking that all paths, especially the one for `react-dom/client`, are correct. I'm reviewing the typecasting of `webkitAudioContext` and adjusting the importmap.


**Updating chat configuration**

I've been analyzing the code and am currently focused on how model parameters are updated within the `chat.sendMessage` call. While direct config overrides might be limited, the `useEffect` hook in `index.tsx` smartly re-initializes the chat with updated configurations, like the `thinkingConfig`, whenever the `thinkingMode` changes. This ensures that new messages adhere to the correct parameters. I'll need to confirm that this approach fully aligns with the intended functionality for on-the-fly model adjustments. I'm assessing the implications of the re-init and how context is handled when the model config changes.


**Validating Imports and Root**

I'm now back at the code, focusing on `index.tsx` and `index.html`. While the prompt focuses on error #299, I also noticed the `config` variable being unused within `sendMessage`. Although it's not the cause of the immediate problem, I'll clean it up to prevent confusion. I'm also confirming the import statements are robust, especially the `react-dom/client` import, and that the root element is validated. I'm now verifying the index.html structure in relation to these imports. I will work on a working example.


**Reviewing Unused Variable**

I've examined the `index.tsx` file in detail, focusing on the `sendMessage` function. While the immediate issue is resolved, I've noticed the `config` variable is defined but not used. It's safe to remove it. Furthermore, I've examined how `thinkingMode` is handled. The code re-initializes `chatRef` with the new configuration when `thinkingMode` changes, so the config is applied correctly. Now, I will verify the code with the previous changes.


**Analyzing Initializations**

I'm now carefully examining the useEffect hook for chat initialization. While the existing code initializes the chat twice on mount, it's functioning as expected, albeit inefficiently. My priority now is to refine and optimize the initializations to ensure consistent behavior with the current architecture. I'm focusing on making a minimal correction. I will also make sure the context of the chat sessions are carried correctly.


**Examining AudioContext Usage**

I'm now carefully reviewing `index.tsx`, especially the `AudioContext` usage within `LiveView`. I see the code employs both `window.AudioContext` and `(window as any).webkitAudioContext` for compatibility. This is the correct approach to handling different browser implementations, ensuring broad support. My immediate focus is to confirm this setup functions reliably across various environments. I'm double-checking that the initialization and usage of the audio context within `LiveView` are robust and do not introduce errors.


**Addressing Script Execution Timing**

My focus is now on ensuring the JavaScript executes after the DOM is fully loaded. I've considered that the script tags might load and execute before the `index.html` structure is complete, especially the `#root` element. To address this, I'm integrating a `DOMContentLoaded` event listener within a `<script>` tag. This ensures the React application initializes only after the DOM is ready, solving a potential race condition and eliminating a source of error. I'll need to confirm that this approach is implemented correctly, ensuring the DOM is initialized before the React application.


**Verifying Script and Root**

I'm now carefully reviewing the suggested `index.html` structure. The script placement at the end of the `<body>` ensures the `root` element exists before React attempts to render. While module scripts are deferred by default, confirming the correct placement reinforces the robustness of the solution. Adding `index.tsx` is essential to demonstrate the full application, including config removal.


**Analyzing Blob and Imports**

I'm revisiting the `index.tsx` file and noting the shadowed `Blob` import from `@google/genai`. While the code utilizes a `createBlob` function, which, while named `Blob` returns an object of a different type, I am checking if there is a conflict. I will make sure the types are correct for the given library. It seems the usage pattern is correct, though.


**Examining Potential Blob Conflicts**

I'm now investigating the potential conflict between the imported `@google/genai`'s `Blob` and the standard browser `Blob` type, specifically within the file handling in the `StudioView`. While it seems the standard `File` object extends the standard `Blob` and is used correctly, I'm checking if the `createBlob` function from `@google/genai` introduces any unexpected behavior due to type shadowing. I'll verify the broader context of how this imported `Blob` interacts with standard DOM types.

**Validating CDN and Structure**

I've been going through the `index.html` file to ensure the CDN paths are correct. The provided import map, specifically the CDN paths, seem valid for the react, react-dom and lucide-react dependencies. I'm now cross-referencing these paths with the current online versions to ensure compatibility. Additionally, I'm ensuring all required scripts are included in the `<head>` section, confirming the necessary modules are available. I will update the code to have everything as correct as possible.


**Verifying Final Touches**

I've just finished the final review of `index.tsx` and the HTML. The unused `config` variable is now gone from the `sendMessage` function, improving code readability. I've also confirmed that the `index.html` structure adheres to the recommendations for CDN paths. I will ensure the type of the `Blob` is correct when generating audio. Now I'm checking that the AudioContext is correctly instantiated.


**Analyzing StudioView Code**

I'm revisiting the `StudioView` code, particularly the `handleEdit` function where image processing happens. I'm focusing on how the `fileToGenerativePart` function converts an image to a format the model understands. The goal is to make sure it functions correctly within the model's structure. I'm checking the output of the function in comparison to the example provided. I'm also reviewing how the response is handled and the state updates after a successful image generation. I will make sure the returned parts are what is expected.


**Constructing XML and files**

I'm now generating the XML to represent the visual layout of the chat interface. I will make a new `index.tsx` based on the old and add the full HTML file, and verify everything works correctly. After the fixes, I can finally create these files. The model parameters for sending are good to go. I'm focusing on making sure all paths are correct.


**Examining Blob Compatibility**

I'm verifying that the `@google/genai` library's `Blob` type is compatible with the standard browser's `Blob` and `File` types within the `createBlob` function. This step is crucial to ensure that the image data is correctly formatted for model processing within `StudioView`, including making `createBlob` work with images. I'm double-checking the code to see if they're handled.

payload.text:
You are a senior frontend engineer and UX designer helping improve an existing nonprofit website called UpwardEase.

About the project
	•	UpwardEase is a nonprofit platform where people can upload their own videos.
	•	The videos are focused on study tips and learning advice for college, high school, and middle school students.
	•	The site currently has:
	•	A video submission area (anyone can upload).
	•	A video gallery (only admin-approved videos are shown).
	•	A weekly featured topic section.
	•	There is or will be an AI assistant, but its role is supporting / assisting, not replacing users’ own content.

⸻

🔹 Overall goals for your code changes

When I paste code files from this project, you must update them with the following goals:
	1.	Visual design & theme
	•	The current site looks too plain.
	•	Make the design feel youthful, hopeful, and study-themed, suitable for students.
	•	Introduce:
	•	A consistent color palette (for example: soft blues/teals, warm accent color, or any harmonious scheme that fits a student mental-health / study-advice theme).
	•	Simple patterns or illustrations / icons related to studying, notebooks, pens, books, light bulbs, etc.
	•	Some small, tasteful micro-interactions (e.g., hover effects on buttons/cards, subtle transitions, maybe a small “floating” accent shape in the background).
	•	Keep it clean, readable, and accessible (good contrast, readable font sizes).
	2.	Clarify the purpose of the site
	•	Make it very clear in the layout and text that the main purpose is:
“A place where anyone can upload their own videos to share study tips and learning advice for college, high school, and middle school.”
	•	Add a short hero section or intro block explaining:
	•	What UpwardEase is.
	•	Who it is for.
	•	What kind of videos people can upload.
	3.	AI is only a helper
	•	Make sure the UI and copy do not imply that AI replaces real students’ voices.
	•	Clearly present AI as:
	•	A tool that can help users organize their thoughts,
	•	Suggest titles, tags, or descriptions for videos,
	•	Or help draft study tips in a friendly way.
	•	Visually separate “AI helper” areas from user-generated content, so it’s obvious AI is supporting the main purpose, not dominating it.
	4.	Weekly featured topic – same for everyone, fun and non-political
	•	The “weekly topic” should:
	•	Be the same for all users within that week (no personalization needed here).
	•	Focus on fun, light, student-friendly themes related to studying, motivation, productivity, or school life.
	•	Avoid political or controversial topics.
	•	Implement or refine a small weekly question / poll / mini-quiz, preferably as a multiple-choice question (MCQ).
	•	Example topics: “Best way to avoid procrastination?”, “Morning or night study person?”, “Favorite note-taking method?”
	•	Each option should be light and relatable for students.
	•	Make sure the UI for this weekly section is clear and visually engaging (e.g., cards or buttons for choices, a short description of the week’s theme).
	5.	Improve overall page structure and layout
	•	Organize the page into clear sections, for example:
	1.	Hero / intro section (what UpwardEase is about).
	2.	“How it works” or “Upload your video” section.
	3.	Video gallery / featured videos.
	4.	Weekly featured topic + MCQ poll/quiz.
	5.	About / footer area.
	•	Use consistent spacing, headings, and typography.
	•	Ensure it is responsive and looks good on desktop, tablet, and mobile.
	•	Avoid unnecessary complexity; keep components reasonably simple, but not boring.
	6.	Footer & rights information
	•	Add or improve a footer that includes at least:
	•	The project name: “UpwardEase”.
	•	A line like:
© UpwardEase {currentYear}. All rights reserved.
	•	A short description (e.g., “A nonprofit platform for students to share study tips and coping experiences.”).
	•	Optional: links or placeholders for “Contact”, “Privacy”, “Terms”.

⸻

🔹 How to work with the existing code
	•	Do not change the overall tech stack (keep using the same framework/library such as React / Next.js / Vue / etc., whatever the current file uses).
	•	Respect the existing data flow and functionality (video upload, gallery, weekly topic logic, AI helper hooks, etc.).
	•	You may:
	•	Refactor components for clarity and reuse.
	•	Improve classNames / styles / layout.
	•	Add new small components (e.g., HeroSection, WeeklyTopicCard, Footer) if it makes the structure cleaner.
	•	Keep the code clean, readable, and well-structured, with meaningful names.

⸻

🔹 Response format

When I paste a file from the project:
	1.	Briefly summarize in 1–2 sentences what the file currently does.
	2.	Then output the fully updated file as a single code block, with the correct language tag (for example tsx or js or jsx depending on the original file).
	3.	Do not add extra commentary outside the code block, unless you need to point out something important about how to integrate it with the rest of the project.

Always make sure your changes follow all six goals above

payload.text: There was an unexpected error. Finish what you were doing.

**Restructuring UpwardEase**

I'm currently focused on restructuring the UpwardEase application. I've been examining the existing React code, particularly how it integrates the `@google/genai` library for AI functionalities. My goal is to streamline the design and improve the overall structure to adhere to the given guidelines. I'm prioritizing efficient code organization and maintainability.


**Enhancing Visual Appeal**

I'm now prioritizing visual enhancements. The color palette is getting a facelift with soft blues, teals, and warm accents for greater consistency. I'm leveraging `lucide-react` more extensively for study-related icons. Typography and spacing will be refined, and I'm adding subtle micro-interactions for a more engaging user experience.


**Organizing the Homepage**

I'm now fully immersed in restructuring the homepage. I'm focusing on the logical flow, starting with a hero section, followed by "How it Works," the weekly topic with a poll, and then showcasing recent videos. The sidebar navigation remains a priority for clarity. I'm also adding a dedicated footer component for copyright and a concise description.



**Implementing Homepage Updates**

My focus has shifted to the `HomeView` and `index.tsx` files. I'm building out the homepage sections: a hero with the title "Share Your Study Journey" and subtitle, followed by the weekly topic and poll. I'm using `ai.models.generateContent` to get the topic and a quiz question. I'm also adding the Footer component to the `App` component now. The main goal is integrating these components with the existing layout.


**Developing New Views**

I'm now focusing on the other views: `StudentView`, `AdminView`, `StudioView`, `LiveView`, and `ResourcesView`. The visual updates are underway, aiming for rounded corners, soft shadows, and consistent icons. I've designed a new `Footer` and a `Hero` component. The `Poll` component is next, which will involve integrating the generation of fun, study-related poll questions. I'm maintaining the existing tech stack, as requested.

**Implementing Visual Updates**

I'm implementing the visual changes across all the views. I'm focusing on rounded corners, soft shadows, and icons to create the youthful aesthetic. The new `Footer` component is complete. I've also finished the new `Hero` component. Now, I'm working on generating poll content.

**Enhancing Visual Themes**

I am now implementing the proposed visual adjustments across the application. The goal is to enhance the study-friendly theme with rounded corners, subtle shadows, and a consistent set of icons. The `Footer` component is complete, and a new `Hero` component has been finalized. Currently, I'm integrating the poll generation logic.


**Improving Homepage Design**

I'm now integrating the updated `HomeView` with the refined logic. The `useEffect` hook will fetch the weekly topic and poll, using the specified prompt to generate JSON data. I'm focusing on ensuring reliable text parsing, despite the JSON structure of the returned data. I'm prioritizing the visual adjustments based on the newly introduced prompts. The `HomeView`'s hero section will feature the welcome message and subtitle. I will integrate the features section and the AI section, too. Finally, I will add the footer at the bottom of the scrollable area.


**Revising Homepage Logic**

I'm now revising the homepage logic, especially in `index.tsx`. I'm focusing on the integration of updated visual elements and prompts. I'm building a scrollable `Main Content Area` with `HomeView` and `Footer`, which includes the "How it Works" section, weekly featured topic, and the call to action for the AI helper. The structure will be Hero -> "How it works" -> Weekly topic -> Footer. I'm refining the text parsing for the weekly topic and poll, even from the JSON structure of data, to ensure a smooth flow.


**Refining Backend Integration**

I'm now integrating the backend. I'll generate the weekly topic from the backend. I'm using `gemini-2.5-flash` for its speed. My adjustments involve `rounded-full` buttons, `rounded-2xl` cards with soft colors, and friendly heading fonts. I've also written the Footer component for the copyright notice and description.


**Constructing the Hero Component**

I'm now focused on the implementation of the `Hero` component. The structure includes decorative elements, the introduction section, and the main title with the subtitle. I've incorporated custom Tailwind classes for the background, text, and decorative elements. The goal is to build a responsive and visually appealing introduction section for the homepage. I'm also integrating Tailwind classes for positioning, padding, and text styling.


**Finalizing Homepage Integration**

I'm now fully integrating the `HomeView` with the revised logic. The weekly topic, and poll will be loaded. I've designed the visual elements to have the buttons for uploading videos and browsing the gallery. The goal is to finish the homepage, including the hero section, the weekly topic with poll, and the footer component, all within the `index.tsx` file.


**Implementing Font Updates**

I'm now integrating the `Nunito` font. I've added the Google Fonts link and updating the Tailwind config to implement `font-sans`, ensuring a consistent and improved visual aesthetic throughout the application.

This is an update to the project to fully implement the requested design overhaul, structural improvements, and feature clarifications.

### Summary of Changes

1.  **Visual Design & Theme**:
    *   **Typography**: Added the "Nunito" font for a friendly, youthful, and approachable feel.
    *   **Color Palette**: Standardized on a `Teal` (calm, growth) and `Yellow` (hope, energy) theme.
    *   **Styling**: Introduced rounded corners (`rounded-2xl`, `rounded-full`), soft shadows, and gradient backgrounds to make the app feel modern and safe.

2.  **Clarified Purpose (Home Page)**:
    *   **Hero Section**: Added a prominent hero banner explicitly stating the platform's mission: "Share your study tips. Find your balance."
    *   **How It Works**: A clear 3-step guide (Record, Upload, Support) to explain the user journey.
    *   **AI as Helper**: Visually distinguished the AI tools as "Assistants" in their own section, reinforcing that they support—not replace—student voices.

3.  **Weekly Featured Topic & Poll**:
    *   Implemented a structured "Weekly Focus" section.
    *   Added a **Mini-Quiz/Poll** component. The app now generates a topic *and* a relevant 3-option poll using Gemini (mocked as "weekly" by generating on load) to engage users without asking for personal data.

4.  **Structure & Layout**:
    *   Refactored the main `App` layout to include a sticky sidebar (desktop) / header (mobile) and a proper **Footer** with copyright and rights information.
    *   Organized `HomeView` into logical vertical sections.

5.  **Component Updates**:
    *   **Student Assistant**: Added a disclaimer about AI limitations and styled the chat bubbles to be softer.
    *   **Studio & Live**: Updated UI containers to match the new card style.

Here are the updated files:

Refactored entire application to meet new design goals, including Hero section, Polls, Footer, and consistent theming.

**Considering Design Options**

I'm currently fleshing out the design options for the React application enhancements. Specifically, I'm focusing on user flows for separate student and admin logins. Next, I'll move into user authentication methods and how these interact with video upload permissions. The goal is a clean, intuitive, and secure experience for all users.


**Expanding Feature Set**

I've been concentrating on the details of the 'video-detail' view now.  I'm adding a comments section under the video display. Also, I'm integrating 'like' and 'favorite' options. I'm prioritizing dark mode toggling as a key user enhancement. Furthermore, I'll ensure the contact link functions properly. I'll document improvements and any outstanding issues in the Debug/Report.


**Integrating New Features**

I'm now implementing the display of the video title, description, and author.  I've added "Like" and "Favorite" toggle buttons, and I'm currently working on the comments section, including a comment list and input form. Furthermore, the refactoring efforts are underway for views, with specific checks for user authentication. The login view is taking shape with tabbed student/admin inputs.


**Developing User Authentication**

I'm now focusing on implementing the mock authentication form. It will accept any non-empty input for simplicity. The main `div` will be wrapped with a class that toggles based on a dark mode state. I'll store comments and likes in local state or mock them, since there is no backend. Finally, I will ensure that the "Contact Us" button correctly calls `setActiveTab('contact')`.


**Implementing UI Components**

I'm now integrating the data access for the `VideoDetailView` by moving the mock video list. I'm also ensuring the application can support a `dark` or `light` theme.  I've defined the necessary state variables, including `user`, `theme`, `view`, and `selectedVideoId`, to control the application's flow and data. Currently, I'm working on the UI design for the login page, focusing on user-friendly input fields and clear navigation between student and admin logins. I will move on to the video detail page after I finish the login design.


**Applying Dark Mode**

I'm now diving into the Dark Mode implementation. I'm swapping out Tailwind classes like `bg-white` and `text-slate-900` to their dark mode counterparts, and updating the index.html and index.tsx files. I'm also refactoring the app structure by creating views for Home, Gallery, and Login, and adding a VideoDetailView. Now, the `videoId` needs a home and I'll be moving the video data and refining the dark mode toggle.


**Preparing XML Data**

I'm now focusing on preparing the necessary XML data structure. I'm considering using an XML data format for the navigation information to enhance the overall application. The existing `Footer` is being updated to accept the `navigate` prop, which will improve the navigation flow of the application. Next, I will refactor the parent elements in order to integrate the data from the mock videos.

Here are the updated files to implement the Login system, Video Details, Comments, Likes/Favorites, Dark Mode, and improved navigation.

### **Summary of Changes**

1.  **Authentication System**:
    *   Created a `LoginView` with separate tabs for **Student** and **Admin**.
    *   Added a global `user` state. Users must log in to upload videos or access the Admin console.
    *   Added a simulated login flow (accepts any email/phone for demo purposes).

2.  **Video Details & Interactions**:
    *   Created `VideoDetailView`. Clicking a video in the gallery now opens this detailed view.
    *   Added **Like** (Thumbs Up) and **Favorite** (Bookmark) buttons with toggle states.
    *   Added a **Comments Section** where logged-in users can post comments.

3.  **Dark Mode**:
    *   Enabled `darkMode: 'class'` in Tailwind config.
    *   Added a toggle button (Sun/Moon) in the Sidebar.
    *   Updated all components with `dark:` variants for colors (backgrounds, text, borders).

4.  **Navigation & Footer**:
    *   The "Contact Us" link in the footer now correctly navigates to the Contact view.
    *   Added breadcrumbs/back buttons in detailed views.

5.  **Debug & Improvements Note**:
    *   **Implemented**: Frontend logic for auth, comments, likes, and dark mode.
    *   **Limitations (Missing)**: Since there is no real backend database, data (comments, likes, login session) will reset when you refresh the page. Phone/SMS verification is simulated. "Rednote" icon uses a placeholder.

### **Code Changes**

entries.description: Enable dark mode class support in Tailwind config.
Implemented Auth, Video Details, Comments, Dark Mode, and improved navigation structure.

payload.text: 1. user can create an account himself using email or phone   2.password should be at a minimum of 8 characters (including one special character and one capitalize letter)   3.renew the layout of the website参考我给的图片 4. debug

**Implementing New Features**

I'm currently focused on the account creation feature. I'm exploring options for email and phone-based registration. I've also started thinking about password requirements. I want to ensure user accounts are secure, requiring a minimum of 8 characters, a special character, and a number. I am now working on how to best implement these password rules.


**Analyzing Design Elements**

I've been examining the reference image's color palette, typography, and visual hierarchy. I'm noting the light cream background with dark text, eggplant-colored CTA buttons, and orange and green accent colors. The typography comparison is next. The current app's font is 'Nunito' (sans-serif), but the reference image uses a serif font for headings. This difference will have an impact on the website. I have to determine whether to match the reference or provide a slight modification for brand consistency.


**Developing Interface Layout**

I'm working on the website's structure, starting with the header. The logo will be top-left, and navigation items will be in the top-right. I'm focusing on the Hero section next, planning for text on the left and an illustration on the right. Finally, I'll design a footer with "Good Morning!" and an arrow.


**Adjusting Navigation Structure**

I'm now revising the app's navigation strategy. Initially, I thought to hybridize the landing page with the dashboard. However, on second thought, the user specifically mentioned they want the layout "renewed" based on the image. So, I will shift the dashboard layout to a Top Navigation Bar, matching the reference image's header. It should provide a cleaner and more consistent user experience, moving away from the sidebar format to enhance the "Thoughtful by nature" vibe.


**Constructing Registration Logic**

I'm currently working on the registration view, adding email/phone, password, and confirmation password inputs. Regex validation is also in progress to ensure strong passwords. I've updated the colors and fonts in the code, transitioning to the cream background and serif headings. I've adjusted the `index.tsx` file, removing the sidebar and adding the new top navigation bar. I've also added the `validatePassword` function and began styling the `HomeView` to match the "BuiltKindly" reference image.


**Crafting Registration Flow**

I'm now implementing the registration process, starting with password validation using the Regex: `^(?=.*[A-Z])(?=.*[!@#$%^&*(),.?":{}|<>]).{8,}$`. I'm updating `index.html` to load fonts and colors. Then I'll adjust `index.tsx` to handle state and navigation: `currentView`, `Navbar` (fixed or static top), `LoginView` (with "Sign up" link), a new `RegisterView`, and a redesigned `HomeView`'s Hero section.

This update completely revamps the application to match the visual style of your reference image (Cream background, top navigation, serif typography) and adds the requested Account Creation and Password Validation features.

### Summary of Changes

1.  **Layout Redesign (Reference Match)**:
    *   **Navigation**: Switched from a Sidebar to a **Top Navigation Bar** to match the "BuiltKindly" reference.
    *   **Theme**: Implemented a "Cream" (`#FDFBF7`) background with "Eggplant" (`#564A75`) primary buttons and Serif typography for headings.
    *   **Hero Section**: Recreated the split layout with large serif text ("Share your tips. Find your balance.") and a friendly illustration style.

2.  **Account Creation & Security**:
    *   **Sign Up Tab**: Added a toggle between "Log In" and "Sign Up" in the auth view.
    *   **Password Validation**: Added strict validation rules:
        *   Minimum 8 characters.
        *   At least one Uppercase letter.
        *   At least one Special character (e.g., !@#$%).
    *   Added visual feedback for password errors.

3.  **Refactoring**:
    *   Removed `Sidebar` code.
    *   Added `Navbar` component.
    *   Updated `HomeView` to match the new aesthetic.

### Code Updates

entries.description: Added Merriweather font for Serif headings and updated Tailwind config with new theme colors (Cream, Eggplant) to match the reference image.
Completely refactored layout to Top Navigation. Implemented Sign Up flow with strict password validation. Updated visual style to match the requested design.

payload.text: 1.focus on upwardsease revise on things nonrelated to upwardsease   see reference in the UpwardsEase ReadMe.docx  2.add user review and our mission to main page (user review can change automatically)

**Defining Brand Alignment**

I'm currently focused on defining the exact brand alignment needed for the `UpwardEase` application update.  Analyzing the mission and branding elements visually presented, I'm beginning to map out the key concepts to integrate. The primary goal is to ensure a seamless and authentic representation of the brand within the application's new content.


**Updating The Narrative**

I've been analyzing the provided image file, specifically focusing on how to translate the existing content within it to accurately reflect the UpwardEase brand. I need to replace the "Thoughtful by nature. Powerful by design." section with something more relevant, drawing inspiration from the user's notes and the application's core mission. Additionally, I'm examining how to integrate the "Our Mission" section and student testimonials seamlessly.


**Formulating Content Integration**

I'm now integrating the "Stressed by school. Supported by peers." headline into the hero section, aiming for a direct connection to student experience.  Considering the need to highlight a sense of community, I am exploring options for a rotating review section, perhaps in the form of a carousel. My focus is still on replacing the generic content and making sure it feels specific to the shared struggle and support aspect that UpwardEase provides. I'm also ensuring the update resonates with the core purpose of the app.


**Crafting New Messaging**

I'm now revising the 'hero' section text to resonate more deeply with the user. The initial goal is to convey a sense of a safe, supportive space for students. I'm focusing on "A safe space for students to vent, share study hacks, and find balance in the academic grind." and updating the call-to-action button, too. Additionally, I'm integrating the provided mission statement into a new section and a rotating testimonial component to add social proof. Lastly, I'm verifying the nav links are updated correctly.


**Adjusting the Mission Statement**

I'm now grappling with the discrepancy between the provided mission statement and the application's core focus, which seems to be more about fostering community and relieving academic pressure. The current mission, about closing the college enrollment gap, feels misaligned. I'll need to adapt it, perhaps by reframing it to emphasize UpwardEase's role in supporting student success within higher education.


**Clarifying the Focus**

I'm now clarifying the focus to ensure the content reflects UpwardEase's core mission: releasing pressure and sharing ideas among students. I'll maintain the layout from the provided screenshots but adapt the text, ensuring it resonates with the application's unique purpose. I'm focusing on "UpwardEase" and translating text from the screenshots to be more relevant. The original mission statement, about closing the college enrollment gap, is not relevant to the new context. I'm adapting the mission text to encompass the idea of creating a digital space for students to release pressure.


**Adding Peer Support Details**

I'm incorporating the new hero title "Overwhelmed by school. Supported by peers." and updating the call-to-action button to "Curious how we help?". I've added a rotating testimonials carousel with reviews from Hannah and Olivia, focusing on the peer support aspects, as I strive to release academic pressure. I also finalized the mission statement rewrite to match the application's actual functionality. I'm making sure to highlight how students are lifting each other up and finding a safe space.


**Constructing the Layout**

I'm now starting on the code, focusing on building out the component structure. I'll insert a `MissionSection` below the Hero, followed by the `TestimonialCarousel`. I'm keeping the "Weekly Focus" and "How It Works" sections for now. The carousel will use `useState` for the active index and `useEffect` for the timer. It'll render the active testimonial. For the `MissionSection`, I'm going with a two-column layout with text and an illustration, which I'll approximate using Lucide icons.
Here are the changes to update the branding to "UpwardEase" specific content, rewrite the hero title, and add the Mission and Testimonials sections.

entries.description: Updated Hero text to be UpwardEase specific. Added Mission section and Auto-rotating User Reviews section to HomeView.
